evidence: Foreground codex-companion task run at xhigh produced a file:line changelog and applied in-place fixes to context.md, reasoning.md, answer.md. The review found and fixed a substantive rigor gap (replaced the refine-and-pull-back existence argument with Monsky's direct basic-segment parity count operating on the ORIGINAL triangles) and unified the 2-adic valuation convention to the additive v throughout (v(2)=1, v(1/2)=-1, v(1/n)=0 for odd n), with |.|_2=2^{-v} carried only as an equivalent restatement (|2|_2=1/2, |T|_2>=2>1, |1/n|_2=1). The companion process exited non-zero only because it terminated a redundant nested self-review it had spawned; the substantive review-and-fix completed before that cleanup. The parent re-read the changelog and re-verified by hand: valuation-convention consistency, valuation-extension existence (Chevalley/Zorn), 3-colouring partition and corner colours, A-point translation invariance (B and C cases), rainbow-area determinant bound v(T)<=-1, the line lemma, the basic-segment Sperner parity count (per-triangle parity, double-count cancellation, odd bottom-edge A/B count), and the v(T)<=-1 vs v(1/n)=0 contradiction. context.md = problem statement only; reasoning.md header-free first-person discovery-order with no meta/source leaks.
